title: Personal Injury Claims Handler
description: Are you a skilled claims professional looking to elevate your career within a dynamic, forward\-thinking team? 360 Search is delighted to represent a leading player in the Irish insurance claims sector as they embark on an exciting phase of strategic expansion. Driven by market shifts and prestigious new insurer partnerships, this organisation is growing its personal injury team in Dublin. As a Personal Injury Claims Handler, you will play a pivotal role in managing a diverse portfolio of injury\-related claims stemming from motor, fleet, private client, and public liability cases. Working within a collaborative, hybrid environment, you will take ownership of the full claim lifecycle, ensuring fair settlements while helping to mentor junior colleagues in a supportive, professional atmosphere. Key Responsibilities * **End\-to\-End Portfolio Management:** Manage a diverse portfolio of personal injury claims originating from motor, fleet, private client, and public liability cases from initial notification through to final settlement. * **Litigation \& Settlement Negotiation:** Proactively negotiate settlements and manage litigation processes, ensuring optimal, cost\-effective outcomes in line with Irish civil liability frameworks. * **MGA \& Insurer Collaboration:** Act as a key liaison between MGAs and insurers, maintaining strong stakeholder relationships and utilising various industry databases and systems to ensure seamless reporting. * **Efficient Cycle Management:** Drive claims progression efficiently, aiming to maintain an average claim lifecycle of 60 days, with damage settlements targeted within the 28\-to\-30\-day range. * **Mentorship \& Support:** Share your expertise to support and mentor junior team members, helping to foster a high\-performance culture focused on continuous development. Required Skills \& Experience * **Irish Claims Expertise:** You must possess a minimum of 3 years of hands\-on experience in personal injury claims handling specifically within the Irish market. * **Litigation \& Negotiation Skills:** Strong practical knowledge of the Irish litigation process and a proven track record of successful settlement negotiations. * **Regulatory \& System Familiarity:** Sound understanding of key insurance databases, industry systems, and Irish regulatory compliance standards. * **Communication \& Collaboration:** Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build trust with claimants, insurers, and legal professionals. * **Dublin\-Based / Hybrid Ready:** Located in or around Dublin, with a desire to work in a hybrid setup (please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this permanent, full\-time position). Nice\-to\-Have * **High\-Volume Claims Management:** Experience managing high\-volume claims portfolios (ideally carrying 150 to 200 active claims per handler). * **Industry Qualifications:** Progress towards or completion of relevant CIP, MDI, or ACII qualifications. What We Offer / Why Join By joining 360 Search’s client on a permanent, full\-time basis, you will benefit from a highly competitive base salary and a comprehensive rewards package. You will enjoy a modern hybrid working structure, with the potential for fully remote working arrangements for the right candidate. Beyond the day\-to\-day, this role offers genuine, structured career progression opportunities within a growing business that values mentorship, professional development, and a healthy work\-life balance.

title: Platform Engineer - AI Drug Discovery Platform
description: 🚀 **Build Software That Accelerates Cancer Drug Discovery** Are you interested in joining a brand\-new engineering team in Dublin working on something genuinely meaningful? Stelfox is exclusively partnering with a **high\-growth, AI\-driven biotech company** that’s transforming how cancer drugs are discovered. This organisation sits at the intersection of **AI, data, and life sciences** —combining cutting\-edge technologies (LLMs, GPUs, cloud platforms) with real\-world clinical data to **redefine oncology research** . 🧑 💻 We’re hiring: **Platform Engineers (Infrastructure Automation / CloudOps / DevOps):** * Build \& operate the platform that runs ML workloads for drug discovery: 🛠️ Tech Stack \& Environment You don’t need everything—but experience across some of the below is key: * Infrastructure as Code (IaC) \- Terraform / OpenTofu * Automate CI/CD pipeline * Cloud \- AWS services * Observability, Logging and monitoring tools * Strong understanding of distributed systems. * Strong Python coding skills. * Experience or exposure to ML workloads or GPUs is a bonus. * Exposure to AI tools (e.g. Claude Code) for day to day engineering. 🎓 Background * 6–10 years’ software engineering experience * Degree (or higher) in Computer Science, Bioinformatics, Computational Biology/Chemistry, Physics, or similar If you would like to be considered for this new team, please feel free to apply for immediate consideration. Please note, visa sponsorship is not provided for these roles.

title: Podiatrist - Nursing Home/ Clinic Cover
description: **Who are we looking for?** For us at Spectrum it’s all about building the best team to help people achieve their health goals. This makes for happy patients and happy team members! We’re currently looking for a Full or Part Time Podiatrist to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team in our clinics and nursing homes in Dublin and Meath. The FTE salary range for this role is €50,000\-€77,000 per annum, with potential earnings of up to €100,000 with commission. As travel would be required as part of this role a drivers license and access to a car would be preferred. At Spectrum we want to offer you a career, not just a job. We provide clear career progression alongside a transparent salary scale to help our team develop clinically or into leadership roles. We look to cater for those who want to build their clinical skills and those who want to upskill to become a Clinical Lead, Manager or Director. Depending on experience, there is an option to deliver nail surgery! **Some reasons why our team joined us:** * Our comprehensive clinical support structure * Clear career pathways across clinical and leadership roles * Work variety – we provide multiple specialist services and provide training in each * Flexible working – we can cater for Full or Part time contracts and shifts to suit your lifestyle * CPD calendar including both internal and external speakers * CPD bursaries to assist funding external courses * Study leave * Robust systems to help our team provide the best care for their patients * Discounted Health Insurance * Annual leave that increases with length of service * Me Day – an additional day leave to try something new * Monthly Health \& Wellness allowance If this sounds like an opportunity you’re interested in, then take a read through the roles and responsibilities and hit apply below. We would love to hear from you! **What is the role and what are your responsibilities?** * Treat a variety of skin ailments such as verruca’s, corns, calluses, etc. * Assess and treat ingrown toenails, clipping toenails and general feet tidy up * Treat patients with diabetes at increased risk of foot disease who require preventative care and assistance * Determine the condition of a patient’s feet, ankles, or lower legs by reviewing his or her medical history, listening to the patient’s concerns, and performing a physical examination * Diagnose foot, ankle, and lower\-leg problems through physical exams, and other methods * Provide treatment for foot, ankle, and lower leg ailments, such as prescribing special shoe inserts (orthotics) to improve and correct foot and ankle deformities * Give advice and instruction on foot and ankle care and on general wellness techniques * Educate patients on how to improve and/or improve conditions * Liaise with other healthcare professionals, such as GP’s, Physios and other members of multi\-disciplinary teams, to exchange information about the background and progress of patients and Refer patients to other physicians or specialists if they detect larger health problems, such as diabetes * Meet continuous education requirements and maintain an up\-to\-date CPD folder * Write patient case notes and reports and collect statistics; as well as managing their payments, and issue receipts * Place stock orders and log repairs * In some cases, supervise student and junior podiatrists and enhance your teaching skills * Manage clinical risk and have excellent infection control awareness * Be caring, compassionate and professional and always have the patient at the core of everything you do * Help with creating blogs and content for social media and other marketing channels * Work with the marketing team and provide them with on the ground feedback from time to time **What experience and qualifications do I need?** * BSc OR MSc in Podiatry (essential) * CORU registration (essential) * Podiatry Ireland Membership (essential) * Professional Indemnity Insurance (essential) * Private practice experience (preferred) * New grads are welcome to apply **If you have any queries about the role, please contact 01 290 2690** Please note that if you click "apply" you consent to your information to be stored for 12 months for recruitment purposes by Spectrum Health.

title: Private Markets Middle Office - Vice President
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Position Summary** KKR is seeking a Middle Office Operations professional for its Global Investment Operations Team based in the Dublin office. This individual will work and collaborate with the global operations team, as well as employees in other functions and businesses at the firm. This role is a unique opportunity to become part of an evolving and strategic function dedicated to "best\-in\-class" operations management practices to support our global business growth. **Responsibilities** * Lead and oversee preparation and review of GAV/NAV and regulatory reporting * Provide governance oversight for daily cash and position reconciliations * Investigate and resolve discrepancies between internal systems and trustees/custodians * Manage and optimize relationships with external service providers * Record and maintain all lifecycle transactions, capital activity, and borrowings in internal systems * Deliver timely, accurate support to external clients and internal stakeholders * Drive transformation initiatives focused on automation, scalability, and operational efficiency * Lead operational governance frameworks and control enhancements * Undertake ad hoc project work and mentor junior team members **Ideal Experience** * 10\+ years of fund accounting experience within asset servicing environment * Strong background in fund administration or operations (credit/asset management, private equity/infrastructure fund industry experience preferred) * Deep knowledge of GAV/NAV calculation on fund vehicles and/or Fund of Fund experience * Proven leadership experience with ability to operate independently and think critically * Client\-facing exposure with strong stakeholder management capabilities * Track record of driving transformation (automation, process improvement, efficiency gains) * Accounting qualification (ACA, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent) strongly preferred * Advanced proficiency in Advent Geneva system * Credit and Private Equity fund structures expertise **CRITICAL COMPETENCIES FOR SUCCESS** * Strategic leadership mindset with clear intellectual curiosity and commercial acumen; drive and potential to succeed in a fast\-paced work environment * Independent self\-starter – someone who enjoys "rolling up his/her sleeves and getting things done"; has high energy; a strong work ethic; displays the ability to work independently; be creative; and entrepreneurial * Critical thinker who grasps key concepts quickly and displays deep specialist knowledge * Exceptional interpersonal and relationship\-building skills – can effectively develop trusted relationships with many constituents, build credibility quickly, and partner effectively with others at all levels * Strong execution and project management skills – highly organized, very detail oriented, proactive problem\-solver, solutions\-oriented, and a knack for anticipating obstacles and addressing issues early on * Efficient and effective work style in a dynamic environment with the ability to multi\-task and work across several different work\-streams, assignments and/or projects. Proven ability to manage heavy workloads while remaining attentive to details, and prioritizing time efficiently when faced with multiple projects * Focuses on delivering exceptional client service, with a sense of urgency and follow through * Displays maturity, outstanding judgment, critical thinking, sensitivity and creativity to changing needs and situations. Acts professionally regarding sensitive subjects and maintains confidentiality while always exercising discretion * Team leader and collaborative partner – willing to mentor and develop others; enjoys working in a collaborative and collegial environment; humble and enthusiastic with continuous learning mindset * Advanced working knowledge of MS Office, particularly advanced Excel, and expert\-level experience with Advent Geneva KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) * Previous office and operational based project engineering \& packaging experience in a Pharmaceutical or FMCG business * Detailed understanding of facility and engineering issues and processes. * A flexible and willing attitude * Communication skills to interact with internal and external customers at a senior level. * Strong Team player with open communication style with the ability to direct and train others and deliver clear instruction, including internal staff, suppliers, and tradesmen. * Design and develop new equipment to help improve line effectiveness. * Ability to influence and collaborate at all levels in order to achieve company targets. * Ability to represent the company in a professional and highly competent manner. * Ability to multitask and prioritise activities. * Ability to work on their own as well as part of a team. * Excellent problem\-solving skills. * Dependable, reliable and shows good integrity. * Ability to travel on company business as required. **Desirable** * Experience in a GMP or Regulated Environment. * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. * Good working knowledge of cGMP, 21CFR Part11, and GAMP “Current”. * Detailed understanding of blister pack tooling design and manufacturing processes. * Use of creating/amending PLC programs. title: Project Engineer - Water
description: **The Vacancy** **About The Company** At Nicholas O'Dwyer, sustainability is at the core of our work. If you are looking to advance your career in a socially responsible company, which has a proven track record over 90 years, and is committed to creating a sustainable planet, we invite you to be part of our team. Our mission is to provide innovative and reliable solutions using cutting edge technology and expertise, to ensure project success and employee satisfaction. We deliver infrastructure projects in water, wastewater, transportation, civil and structural engineering, flood protection, and energy including renewables. With headquarters in Ireland, we also operate in the UK, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. As part of the RSK Group, an amalgamation of over 200 companies with more than 17,000 employees, we benefit from a global network of opportunities while retaining our brand and autonomy. **About The Role** We are seeking a Project Engineer – Water to support the delivery of water infrastructure projects across Ireland, the UK, and internationally, with a particular focus on utility clients, and opportunities to contribute to projects across a wider portfolio depending on business needs and individual interest. The role involves contributing to and progressing projects from initial brief through to completion, supporting the delivery of projects on programme, within budget, and to the required technical and quality standards. The successful candidate will work closely with Senior Engineers, Project Managers, and multidisciplinary teams to deliver high\-quality engineering solutions while continuing to develop their technical and project management capabilities. **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Support the delivery of water infrastructure projects from feasibility and concept design through to detailed design, procurement, construction, and completion, ensuring delivery to programme, budget, and quality requirements. * Preparation of Feasibility Studies, Route and Site Selections, and Concept Design Reports. * Preparation of Outline and Detailed Designs, including drawings, calculations, and technical reports. * Prepare Technical Specifications and Contract Documents for main works and investigation contracts. * Support Tender Evaluation and Procurement processes. * Assist in the preparation of documentation required for statutory approvals, including planning permissions, land acquisition, and wayleaves. * Support construction stage contract administration, including site inspections and compliance monitoring. * Assist in the development of project plans, including programme scheduling, budgets, and resource planning. * Coordinate with project teams, clients, and stakeholders to support effective project delivery. * Contribute to technical delivery on assigned projects, ensuring compliance with applicable standards, specifications, and contractual requirements. * Attend client, stakeholder, and technical meetings and contribute to project discussions in consultation with senior staff. * Prepare technical reports, designs, calculations, drawings, and specifications for review by senior engineers. * Assist with project cost tracking, programme monitoring, and change management activities. * Identify project risks and support the implementation of mitigation measures. * Coordinate and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, including environmental, planning, and other technical disciplines. * Maintain effective communication on project progress, risks, and technical matters internally and with clients. * Perform site visits and inspections to monitor project progress, quality, and adherence to specifications. * Support and collaborate with junior team members while continuing to develop technical expertise and professional competencies. * Undertake additional duties as required to support project delivery and reflect the evolving needs of the business. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in civil engineering, Environmental Engineering, or a related discipline. * 5 years’ experience in water engineering, including aspects of feasibility studies, detailed design, procurement, and/or construction contract administration. * Experience in the design and delivery of water infrastructure projects such as treatment plants, pumping stations, pipelines, and water networks. * Experience working with Uisce Éireann and/or water utility companies in the UK and Ireland is desirable. * Strong technical knowledge of water engineering principles, standards, regulations, and industry best practice. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities and meet project milestones. * Clear written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively in client\-facing environments. * Experience working effectively within multidisciplinary teams and contributing to integrated project delivery. * Working toward professional chartership (e.g., Engineers Ireland or equivalent) is desirable. * Full clean driving license essential, with flexibility to travel regularly to project sites as required. * Ability to work effectively within multidisciplinary teams and contribute to integrated project delivery. **About Us** Our mission is to deliver sustainable solutions that transform communities and bring about lasting positive change. Through a blend of expertise, innovation and global dedication, we envision a world where sustainable engineering protects growing communities. Nicholas O’Dwyer provides professional services in engineering design, project management, environment and planning, construction supervision and technical and contract advisory. We are one of the most respected Irish consultancies operating in local and international markets, providing professional services to public\- and private\-sector clients. Whether in Ireland, the UK or internationally, we understand and solve major challenges associated with delivering and adapting infrastructure for an increasing global population while maintaining a sustainable environment for future generations. Nicholas O’Dwyer is part of the RSK Group, a leading integrated environmental, engineering, and technical services business providing dynamic, multidisciplinary services to a wide range of sectors. RSK was founded in 1989 and has grown considerably since then. At RSK, we operate as a group of businesses that work together seamlessly, aiming for growth through diversification. Our strategy helps us build a strong and resilient business, capable of weathering external challenges, while also providing greater value to our clients and more opportunities for our employees. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: Purchasing Administrator
description: **About O'Briens Wines** O'Briens Wines is Ireland's leading family\-owned drinks retailer, with 35 stores nationwide and over 1,000 wines sourced directly from more than 180 wineries worldwide. We are passionate about exceptional products and outstanding customer experiences \- whether in\-store, online, or through our growing digital channels. Across our stores, Support Office and online operations, our teams work in a collaborative, supportive environment built on trust, teamwork and genuine care. At O'Briens, our people bring our brand to life. **Role Overview** **Contract Type:** Permanent, Full\-Time **Location:** Support Office, Sandyford, Dublin \& Remote **Reporting to:** Product Team **Salary:** Competitive, commensurate with experience **This role would suit a recent graduate with a logical mindset and strong systems aptitude who is looking to build a career in purchasing and commercial operations \- full training on our processes and systems will be provided** **Role Purpose** As the business grows, the volume of item data administration in our ERP system (Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central) has placed increasing demands on our buyers. This new role will centralise all item data management, freeing our buyers to focus on sourcing, supplier negotiation, and commercial strategy. The Purchasing Administrator will be the single point of ownership for the accuracy, completeness, and integrity of product data in Business Central. This is a high\-trust, high\-precision role that requires someone who is systematic, assertive, and genuinely comfortable working within a structured ERP environment. **Key Responsibilities** **Item \& Product Data Management** * Own the end\-to\-end setup and maintenance of product records in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. * Create and activate new items, ensuring all mandatory data is complete and accurate before release. * Maintain product attributes, barcodes, GTINs, vendor information and item status. * Enforce data quality standards, returning incomplete requests and preventing inaccurate data from entering the system. * Liaise with buyers and suppliers to obtain missing information and secure approvals. **Pricing, Promotions \& Rebates** * Process cost and retail price changes accurately and within agreed timelines. * Maintain promotional calendars and support promotional activity, including store\-specific campaigns. * Produce and maintain competitor pricing reports. **Compliance \& Data Governance** * Maintain the organic certification database, monitor certificate renewals and ensure legal compliance. * Conduct regular audits of product certification and promotional data. **Order Processing \& Team Support** * Coordinate complex multi\-drop corporate orders using Business Central configuration packs. * Support buyers with product administration, commercial events and promotional data. * Provide general administrative support to the Products team while acting as the gatekeeper for product data quality. **Essential** * Strong logical and systematic approach, with a focus on accuracy and process compliance. * Confident and assertive in enforcing processes, even when challenged by buyers, suppliers, or colleagues under pressure. * Comfortable holding firm when required to protect data integrity and business standards. * Understands the downstream impact of poor item setup across buying, finance, and sales, and takes ownership for getting it right. * Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, data validation, structured tables, bulk data manipulation). * Experience using an ERP system (Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central preferred). * Exceptional attention to detail with a strong commitment to data accuracy. * Ability to manage multiple priorities and competing deadlines. * Strong written and verbal communication skills for supplier and cross\-functional collaboration. **Desirable** Experience in a retail, FMCG, drinks, or food \& beverage business Familiarity with LS Retail, Shopify, or other point\-of\-sale/e\-commerce systems Understanding of promotional mechanics, rebate structures, or supplier funding models Background or interest in data science, analytics, or AI \- we are actively investing in data capabilities and welcome candidates who can contribute to that agenda Experience with document management, process documentation, or SOPs **What We Offer You:** **Benefits:** Enjoy Employee discounts on wine and fizz for all our colleagues. Take advantage of our **Bike to Work** scheme. Access **HSF Health Plans**, covering healthcare expenses including dental, optical, and more. Participate in our **Company Pension Scheme** for future financial security. Benefit from our **Refer a Friend** scheme. Explore a variety of **Development Opportunities** through both online and classroom\-based learning, delivered by in\-house and external industry experts. **Training \& Growth:** Take part in **WSET Wine and Spirit Courses** to expand your wine knowledge and earn recognized qualifications in the industry. We offer excellent **career progression** opportunities, with many of our promotions being filled internally. **Our Commitment to Inclusion:** We believe every colleague should feel empowered to be their authentic self at work respected, valued, and included, no matter who they are, where they're based, or what role they play. Inclusion is at the heart of how we operate. When we embrace individuality and diverse perspectives, we foster a sense of belonging and quite simply, diverse teams make better decisions and drive greater impact. As a company, we are continuously expanding, but it's the dedication and talent of our people that drives our success. At O'Brien's, we believe in fostering talent and providing ongoing learning opportunities to help you advance in your career. If you're motivated, ready to learn, and excited about working in a vibrant and busy environment, there's plenty of room to grow with us.

title: ROS Engineer
description: We'd love to have you be part of the MūL team! **ROS Engineer for Autonomous Mobile Robots** **Position Title:** AMR Engineer (ROS 1 \& ROS 2\) **Location:** Mequon, WI **Reports To:** CTO **Job Type:** Full\-Time **Position Summary** We are seeking a skilled and knowledgeable ROS Engineer with expertise in both ROS 1 and ROS 2 to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have hands\-on experience with autonomous mobile robots, demonstrating a strong understanding of robotics software frameworks and practical applications. This role involves developing, testing, and deploying advanced robotics solutions for our cutting\-edge projects. This position is facility\-based in Mequon, WI. Some travel to customer sites is required. **Key Responsibilities** **Development and Integration:** * Design and develop software components for autonomous mobile robots using ROS 1 and ROS 2\. * Integrate sensors, LIDAR, depth sensing cameras, and other hardware components with ROS\-based systems. * Implement navigation, perception, and control algorithms for mobile robots. **Testing and Debugging:** * Conduct rigorous testing of software modules to ensure reliability and performance. * Debug and troubleshoot issues related to robotics software and hardware integration. * Optimize system performance and ensure real\-time capabilities of robotic applications. **Research and Innovation:** * Stay updated with the latest advancements in ROS and autonomous robotics. * Propose and implement innovative solutions to enhance the capabilities of autonomous mobile robots. * Participate in research projects and contribute to publications and patents. **Qualifications** **Education:** * At least a Bachelor's degree in Robotics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. **Experience:** * Proven experience with ROS 1 and ROS 2 in a professional or academic setting. * Hands\-on experience with autonomous mobile robots, including navigation, perception, and control. * Familiarity with robotics simulation tools such as Gazebo, RViz, and simulation environments. **Technical Skills:** * Proficiency in C\+\+, Python, and other relevant programming languages. * Strong understanding of robotic systems, kinematics, and sensor integration. * Experience with SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) and path planning algorithms. * Proficiency in Docker and GitHub **Soft Skills:** * Excellent problem\-solving and analytical skills. * Strong communication and teamwork abilities. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with machine learning and computer vision applications in robotics. * Previous experience in an agile development environment. **Benefits** * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Opportunity to work on innovative and impactful projects. * Professional development and growth opportunities. * Collaborative and inclusive work environment. **Application Process** Interested candidates are invited to submit their resume, cover letter, and any relevant project portfolios or publications to \[contact email]. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. **Company Overview** MūL Technologies is a manufacturer of MARC (Mobile Autonomous Robotic Cart) AMRs, an easy\-to\-use self\-guided robotic cart that combines the benefits of AMRs and AGVs. MARC is different than other AMRs on the market because: * It requires no Wi\-Fi or other connection to operate. * MARC can be implemented in under 10 minutes in any facility. * It is truly autonomous, and completely self\-contained. * Pricing for MARC and associated products is simple and available on our online shop. The company was founded to offer simple cost\-effective robotic solutions for businesses everywhere, tagged “Robotics for the Rest of Us.” By using commercial off\-the\-shelf (COTS) components and adding minimal custom hardware, we created the most cost\-effective AGR solution on the market. MūL Technologies is headed by an experienced team of programming, engineering, manufacturing, logistics and sales professionals with a combined 200 years of expertise and is backed by GMI Solutions, a Contract Manufacturing firm started in the Midwest in 1983\. MARC is the most cost\-effective autonomous material handling solution on the market. Finally “the rest of us” have an affordable way to get all the benefits of autonomous robots. If this sounds interesting, we want to hear from you! Email your info to work4us@multechnologies.com. REACH OUT Use this form to get the ball rolling. We'll reach out to discuss the possibilities with you! **Call** 262\.242\.8830 **Email** work4us@multechnologies.com Let us know you're interested! Email\* First Name Last Name Job you are interested in UploadUpload your resume, along with any supporting documentation. MessageTell us anything else you think we should know.

title: Receptionist
description: **Company Description** **Our heart beats turquoise.** Working at Motel One is like coming home to friends, where you can be your true self and where appreciation and support matter. We meet on an equal footing and see ourselves as one team: from our management to our trainees. We create a home away from home for our guests with our shared passion for modern design and high\-quality service. Our more than 100 hotels in 14 countries impress with exclusive design, high service standards and first\-class inner\-city locations at an attractive price. As the founder and pioneer of the budget design concept, we are successfully expanding in the metropolises of Europe and beyond. Become part of our team and develop Motel One with us. **Job Description** As a Receptionist, you are the first and last impression of our hotel, placing the guest at the centre of everything you do. You will deliver a warm, genuine and professional welcome, ensuring every interaction creates a positive and memorable experience. From managing check\-ins and check\-outs to responding to guest enquiries and resolving requests with care and efficiency, you will provide exceptional service while working collaboratively with your colleagues to exceed guest expectations. You will take pride in creating a welcoming atmosphere, anticipating guests' needs and ensuring every stay reflects the high standards and hospitality of Motel One. **Qualifications** * Previous experience in a hotel reception or customer service role is desirable. * Experience using Opera Cloud or a similar Property Management System (PMS) is an advantage, although full training will be provided. * A warm, friendly and professional personality with a genuine passion for delivering exceptional guest service. * A positive attitude, strong communication skills and the ability to remain calm and organised in a fast\-paced environment. * A proactive team player who takes pride in creating memorable guest experiences. * Strong attention to detail and the ability to multitask while maintaining high standards of service. * Must be eligible to work in Ireland and currently resident in Ireland. **Additional Information** At Motel One Dublin, we believe our people are at the heart of everything we do. We work as **One Team** , supporting each other, celebrating successes together and creating a positive, fun and welcoming workplace where everyone can thrive. While we take great pride in delivering exceptional guest experiences, we also believe work should be enjoyable. We recognise and reward outstanding performance through a **generous quarterly bonus scheme** , based on our guest satisfaction scores and service excellence. This means that when we delight our guests, our team shares in the success. If you're looking for a workplace where your attitude, personality and passion for hospitality are valued just as much as your experience, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Recruiter
description: **Title: Recruiter, EMEA** **Contract:29/06/2026 to 23/12/2026** **Work location: Dublin, IRL** **Job Description** Futureforce is the client's global university recruitment program. The program is dedicated to attracting, retaining, and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We are seeking a customer\-centric Talent Delivery Recruiter to join our dynamic team. As the Talent Delivery Recruiter, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the successful recruitment of intern, apprentice and graduate candidates by overseeing the entire interview, selection and offer processes. Your expertise in working alongside Talent Attraction to identify top tier talent and managing the recruitment lifecycle will directly contribute to the optimisation, growth and success of our organisation. **Your Impact** * You will play a critical role in optimising the talent acquisition process by ensuring that candidates are **well\-matched** to job roles, thoroughly informed/ **coached** , and engaged throughout the hiring journey * Your focus on candidate **advocacy** and relationship\-building will help to enhance the candidate experience, thereby increasing the chances of securing top talent for the organisation * Additionally, your collaboration with the Talent Attraction team, hiring managers and Centre of Excellence (CoE) leads will ensure successful **conversion** of top talent plus that the hiring process is efficient, effective and aligned with the organisations strategic objectives **Desired Skills/Experience:** * Experience: 3 \+ years of significant, demonstrable experience in talent acquisition, recruitment, or related HR functions * Customer centricity: A track record of building strong relationships with customers and stakeholders to fully understand motivation factors, align agreed actions/strategies and deliver customer success * Ability to partner and influence key stakeholders at all levels, including senior executives, to drive hiring goals and program outcomes * Data\-driven, with expertise in KPI management, pipeline management, and the ability to meet quarterly hiring targets. * Understanding of Talent Assessment: Proficient in evaluating candidates' skills, qualifications, behaviours and potential for success in different roles * Interpersonal Skills: Exceptional communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills to build rapport with candidates and stakeholders * Coaching Abilities: Demonstrable ability to coach and guide candidates and hiring managers through the hiring process, offering valuable data insights and providing constructive timely and impactful feedback * Results\-Driven: Proven track record of delivering results and meeting recruitment goals within established timelines. * Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced and dynamic environment, adapting to changing priorities and requirement * Fluent in English

title: Registered Architect
description: Location: Dublin/Waterford Employer's Address Respond Airmount, Dominick Place, Waterford X91A397 Closing Date: 10 Jul 2026 Employer's Website https://www.respond.ie/ Employer's Email Info@respond.ie Job Description Location: Dublin / Waterford / Hybrid Reporting To: Design Lead \& Head of Development Hours: 39 hours per week Terms: Full time permanent Salary Range: €69,566 to €90,027 (Please note that offers are typically made at the beginning to mid\-range of the advertised salary, based on skills and experience) Purpose of the Role The Senior Architect plays a key leadership role in shaping Respond’s development programme and ensuring the highest standards of design and delivery across residential and community projects. The role leads architectural design, manages Public Works Contract (PWC) schemes, and provides expert guidance to Respond internal teams as a member of the Design sub\-division of the Development Department team including liaising with Housing, Finance, Health and Safety and Construction teams and external and internal delivery partners. The post holder supports the Executive Head of Development in delivering annual housing targets and ensuring alignment with Respond’s Corporate Strategy, Design Guide, and ESG Strategy. The Senior Architect acts as an in‑house senior design professional across multiple residential, community, public realm and mixed‑use projects, ensuring design quality, compliance, sustainable construction practices and value for money across all schemes. Significant experience in the design and evaluation of complex urban/suburban residential schemes and the ability to lead design reviews under technical merit/qualitative criteria is essential. Key Responsibilities 1\. Architectural Design \& Delivery * Translate briefs into feasible design concepts and lead design development from sketch to completion * Manage PWC projects including feasibility, funding applications, tendering, contract administration, and onsite delivery * Prepare business cases, feasibility studies, and funding proposals * Conduct design due diligence, risk assessment, and stakeholder negotiation * Ensure all projects reflect Respond’s design principles, sustainability goals, and placemaking standards * Evaluate development submissions and design responses for technical merit and quality under Responds designated quality criteria 2\. Design Leadership \& Quality * Act as Senior Architect on residential, community, childcare, amenity, conservation, refurbishment, landscaping and public realm projects liaising with Housing, Asset management, Health and Safety and other internal teams in Respond as required * Lead design teams and external consultants; oversee internal design reviews * Embed inclusive design, universal design, lifetime homes standards, and low‑carbon principles in all development schemes * Support innovation including MMC, NZEB compliance, ecology, biodiversity, and sustainable construction * Develop tools, systems, and procedures to strengthen design quality and consistency 3\. Stakeholder \& Client Engagement * Present design proposals both internally and externally to stakeholders and negotiate with both consultants and contractors * Support internal and external partners including local authorities, the Housing Agency, and HFA * Lead internal design review workshops and ensure future‑proofing and sustainability principles are applied 4\. Public Works Contract Delivery * Support procurement and appointment of design teams, contractors, and advisors * Ensure compliance with CWMF, PWC requirements, and procurement rules * Review development agreements, building contracts, and legal documentation * Monitor consultant performance and address issues or disputes * Review tender documentation, construction drawings, and design submissions 5\. Technical Oversight * Lead design on reviews, verify constructability, and ensure building regulations compliance, and attend site meetings as required * Oversee tender processes, contractor appointments, and consultant fee recommendations * Provide architectural and contractual advice to delivery teams including technical assessments * Support Senior Development Managers on community and specialist buildings * Contribute to research on sustainability, MMC, ecology, biodiversity, and low‑carbon design 6\. Quality, Compliance \& Reporting * Maintain accurate documentation including planning, funder, consultant, and contractor agreements * Prepare timely internal and external reports * Ensure compliance with legislation, building regulations, procurement rules, and health and safety requirements * Maintain records using Respond’s IT systems (ActiveH, AutoCAD, BIM tools) * Ensure O\&M manuals, safety files, and handover documentation are complete 7\. Financial \& Contract Management * Assist in procurement and tendering for new build projects * Coordinate external consultants and manage project budgets, logs, and progress tracking Support cost management, value‑for‑money assessments, and financial reporting 8\. Leadership \& Team Development * Contribute to strategic planning and decision‑making within the Development Department * Build strong relationships with government bodies, local authorities, developers, funders, and contractors * Mentor junior architects and designers; support recruitment and professional development. * Ensure high standards of client care and organisational representation Job Requirements Experience \& Qualifications: Essential * Master’s degree in architecture * RIAI Registered Architect * 10\+ years post‑qualification experience including social housing design and delivery * Strong experience with PWC projects, CWMF compliance, contract administration, and design reviews * Comprehensive knowledge of the Irish Planning System, Building Regulations, and construction delivery * Expertise in low‑carbon and energy‑efficient design, biodiversity enhancement, and sustainable construction an advantage * Strong IT skills: AutoCAD, BIM (Revit/Archicad), MS Office, 3D rendering tools * Proven leadership, negotiation, and stakeholder‑management skills * Experience in residential, community, and mixed‑use design Desirable * Postgraduate qualification in conservation, sustainability, urban design, landscaping , MMC or the public realm, Master planning in an urban/ suburban context. * Experience with PPP projects, strategic procurement, and cost management * Experience with AHBs, local authorities, or public bodies * Evidence of continued professional development in legislation, construction regulations, safety, procurement, and contract administration Personal Attributes * Strong leadership and team‑management capability * Analytical, detail‑oriented, and comfortable interpreting complex data * Motivated, passionate, and committed to high‑quality design * Customer‑focused with strong negotiation and influencing skills * Excellent organisational, planning, and problem‑solving abilities

description: **About Us** McSport is Ireland's leading and most successful independent online supplier of sport and fitness equipment. We are a 100% Irish owned, second\-generation family business. With a team of over 60 industry experts, we are committed to supplying our customers with the highest standard of product, care and service. **Job Purpose** **McSport is currently looking for a Sales Associate** to join ourshowroom. We are looking for energetic, hardworking individuals who are passionate and dedicated in what we do to bring all their expert knowledge to our progressive business! **Duties \& Responsibilities:** * Ensure the Customer Journey from start to finish is a 5\-star experience. * Dealing with sales returns, complaints \& Issues within the showroom. * Ensure all Quotes are followed up by phone/email in a timely manner. * Process Payments \- Cash / Realex / PayPal / Vouchers etc. * Manage and grow our relationship with our current customers. * Log warranties for Cardio Machines. * General Administration Duties. **Key Skills \& Experience:** * Minimum 1\-year experience in the retail industry. * Personal Training qualification a distinct advantage. * Be customer focused. * Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written. * Ability to work under pressure and on your own initiative. * Working knowledge of the sports and fitness industry. **Why Work for Us!** We hire exceptional people and equip them with autonomy, trust, and resources. title: ServiceNow Architect
description: **Permanent Full Time Role** **Can be based in either Ireland or UK** **Key Responsibilities** * Define and maintain the overall architecture of the ServiceNow platform. * Lead the design and implementation of core ITSM modules (Incident, Problem, Change, Request, CMDB). * Architect and oversee the development of a comprehensive reporting and analytics framework using Performance Analytics, Reporting, and Dashboards. * Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into scalable solutions. * Ensure platform governance, security, and performance best practices are followed. * Design and oversee integrations with third\-party systems (e.g., monitoring tools, HR, finance). * Develop a roadmap for future module adoption (ITOM, ITBM, HRSD, CSM, etc.). * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to ServiceNow developers and administrators. * Conduct architectural reviews and ensure alignment with enterprise IT strategy. * Stay current with ServiceNow releases and recommend adoption strategies. **Required Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience as a ServiceNow Architect in enterprise environments. * Deep understanding of ServiceNow platform capabilities and limitations. * Strong experience with ITSM processes and ServiceNow modules. * Expertise in ServiceNow reporting tools including Performance Analytics, Scheduled **Reports, And Dashboards** * Experience with scripting (JavaScript, Glide), Flow Designer, and IntegrationHub. * Familiarity with ServiceNow data models, CMDB design, and service mapping. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. * ServiceNow Certified System Administrator and Certified Application Developer (preferred). * ServiceNow Certified Technical Architect (highly desirable). **Uniphar – Who We Are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties **.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Site Engineer - Electrical
description: **About the Role** Ethos Engineering is looking for a technically skilled and motivated **Electrical Site Engineer** to join our team and support the delivery of high\-profile projects across Ireland and EMEA. This role is ideal for someone with a strong electrical background who thrives in a site\-based environment and is eager to work on a diverse range of projects including data centres, commercial builds, industrial developments, and fit\-outs. You’ll play a key role in ensuring high\-quality electrical installations that align with design intent, safety standards, and project timelines. Flexibility and willingness to travel are essential, as this role will involve working across multiple sites throughout Ireland and potentially Europe. **About You** * Degree qualified or certified in Electrical Engineering or a related field * Proven experience in electrical site engineering, preferably within building services * Strong understanding of electrical systems: LV/MV distribution, emergency lighting, cable containment, fire alarm systems, etc. * Confident communicator with excellent coordination and reporting skills * Detail\-focused, organised, and able to manage deadlines on active construction sites * A proactive mindset and strong problem\-solving abilities * Comfortable mentoring junior engineers and collaborating with multidisciplinary teams * Full driving licence and flexibility to travel across project sites in Ireland and EMEA **Key Responsibilities** **Electrical Installation \& Quality Oversight** * Review and interpret electrical design drawings, specifications, and installation benchmarks * Attend FATs and witness testing to ensure products meet technical requirements * Carry out system walkdowns, snagging inspections, and maintain snag/defect lists * Check all electrical installations align with design intent, safety codes, and industry best practice * Oversee QA/QC of electrical packages, including LV/MV systems, emergency lighting, fire alarm, security systems, and containment **Site Coordination \& Reporting** * Liaise with electrical contractors, subcontractors, and commissioning teams * Maintain a detailed site diary, record progress, and escalate risks or issues to project leads * Monitor subcontractor performance and ensure timely reporting to project planners and managers * Use tools like ProCore and Zutec for documentation, variation orders, and compliance uploads * Ensure site documentation, records, and installation drawings are up to date and accurate **Safety, Leadership \& Team Support** * Adhere to and enforce Health \& Safety regulations across all site activities * Support the site safety manager in implementing safe systems of work * Develop strong professional relationships with site personnel and stakeholders * Mentor and support junior engineers through technical guidance and knowledge sharing * Contribute to team development and continuous improvement across project delivery. ##### **Why Join Us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing**. **We offer:** * **Flexible Fridays** and a **9\-day fortnight working schedule** — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A culture that values **flexibility, learning, and teamwork**, with clear progression pathways and support toward **professional accreditation**. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the **most experienced engineers** in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times * 20 Days holidays up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Support for Chartership * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) **Company Description:** Ethos is a team of collaborative and creative consultant engineers \& professionals, leading data centre and energy infrastructure projects globally. **Our Charter:** * We believe in strong human relationships * We believe clear leadership lights the path * We believe sustainability is non\-negotiable * We believe a can\-do mindset can move mountains * We believe in a relentless focus on delivery **Our Tagline:** Performance Made Possible
